items.title: Kane leads England to statement win over Croatia
items.summary: England coach Thomas Tuchel said his players overcame their nerves and the psychological fear of losing a lead to power past Croatia at Dallas Stadium (AT&T Stadium) on Wednesday.

items.title: Portugal boss defends loyalty to misfiring Ronaldo...
items.summary: Congo DR produced another stunning result at the 2026 World Cup, as the African side held one of the pre-tournament favorites, Portugal, to a 1-1 draw in Houston on Wednesday.

items.title: NYPD to deploy 10K officers for Knicks title parad...
items.summary: The victory parade for the NBA champion New York Knicks will be safeguarded by 10,000 members of the NYPD, the largest number of police officers ever assigned to a planned event, the department said Wednesday.

items.title: Cincinnati denies claim it knew of Sorsby's bettin...
items.summary: Cincinnati on Wednesday, responding to Brendan Sorsby's agent publicly questioning the school's handling of the quarterback's gambling in 2024, said it would never knowingly play an athlete who violated NCAA gambling rules.

items.title: 'Debut album': Clark unveils signature Nike shoe
items.summary: Caitlin Clark announced her first signature shoe -- the Caitlin 1 -- on Wednesday afternoon.

items.title: Ohtani battles blister, sore knee in rocky outing
items.summary: Pitching with a sore left knee and a blister on his right middle finger, Shohei Ohtani cruised through four innings before allowing four runs in a rocky fifth inning, but the Dodgers rallied to beat the Rays 5-4.

items.title: Tigers' Perez hurt when resistance band hits face
items.summary: Tigers outfielder Wenceel Perez was injured in a freak accident when he was hit in the the face by a resistance band in the training room following Tuesday's game against the Astros.

items.title: Your cousin from Scotland: Fans drink Boston dry
items.summary: The parent company of Sam Adams said its Boston Taproom ran out of the brand's flagship Boston Lager over the weekend because Scotland soccer fans drank four times as much as it typically sells.

items.title: ⛳ Top U.S. Open storylines from Shinnecock Hills
items.summary: Is Scottie Scheffler still the favorite? How hard will Shinnecock Hills play? We break down what to watch at the 2026 U.S. Open.

items.title: Brunson, Hart toss first pitches at Yankee Stadium...
items.summary: Knicks stars Jalen Brunson and Josh Hart received loud ovations from fans at Yankee Stadium as they took the field to throw out the ceremonial first pitches before Wednesday's White Sox-Yankees game.
